notes: Bronze rules for the May 2024 OEWS National wage-percentile ingest (~830 detailed SOCs). Threshold for wage_annual_median non-null tightened from spec's 95% to 99% per EDA evidence (actual 99.398%, 5/831 suppressed in performance-arts SOCs). DELIBERATELY NO RULE for `wage_annual_mean <= wage_annual_p90` -- 23 top-coded SOCs (Cardiologists, Anesthesiologists, etc.) report mean > p90 because BLS publishes the mean uncapped while p90 is floored at $239,200. Documented in EDA §Anomalies and domain-context.md as expected; flagging would generate 23 false positives every refresh.

rules.name: Row count within expected range: 800-900
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 800 OR cnt > 900 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM bronze.bls_oews)
rules.description: BLS OEWS publishes ~830 detailed occupations after filtering OCC_GROUP='detailed'. Row count outside [800, 900] indicates a download failure, filter regression (summary-row leakage), or source-format change.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 1 DQ Rules: '800 <= count <= 900 (P0 -- ~830 expected detailed occupations).' EDA: 'Row count is in spec range (831, target 800-900). Filter on OCC_GROUP == detailed is working -- no major/minor/broad rollup leakage.' Threshold preserved as spec-stated.

rules.name: SOC code format: XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bls_oews WHERE soc_code IS NULL OR NOT regexp_matches(soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}$')
rules.description: Every soc_code must match the SOC 2018 format XX-XXXX (two digits, hyphen, four digits). Any deviation indicates a parsing or type-coercion failure (e.g. hyphen stripped, leading zero dropped).
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 1 DQ Rules: 'SOC code format: matches XX-XXXX regex (P0).' EDA: 'All match ^\d{2}-\d{4}$ (0 violations).' Domain context: SOC code format is the primary join key to OOH and O*NET. Threshold: 0 violations (100% conformance).

rules.name: Grain uniqueness: soc_code
rules.sql: SELECT soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM bronze.bls_oews GROUP BY soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared dedup grain is [soc_code]. Any duplicate soc_code indicates an ingestor dedup failure or summary-row leakage (e.g. detailed code colliding with a stripped major-group code).
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 1 DQ Rules: 'SOC code uniqueness (P0).' EDA: '831 distinct (100% unique).' Threshold: 0 duplicates.

rules.name: wage_annual_median non-null rate >= 99%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(non_null AS DOUBLE) / CAST(total AS DOUBLE)) < 0.99 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, SUM(CASE WHEN wage_annual_median IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS non_null FROM bronze.bls_oews)
rules.description: wage_annual_median must be non-null for at least 99% of rows. Tightened from spec's 95% per EDA: actual is 99.398% (826/831). Five performance-arts SOCs (27-2011 Actors, 27-2031 Dancers, 27-2042 Musicians/Singers, 27-2091 DJs Except Radio, 27-2099 Entertainers/Performers All Other) are intrinsically suppressed by BLS because gig compensation is not meaningfully measurable as an hourly/annual wage.
rules.rationale: EDA §Threshold Lock-In: 'Spec proposed >= 95% non-null on wage_annual_median; actual 99.398%, recommend tightening to >= 99% to leave room for one extra suppression next refresh while detecting regression.' Spec-deviation: tightened from spec's 95% threshold to 99% per EDA evidence-based recommendation.

rules.name: Per-row monotonicity: p10 <= p25 <= median <= p75 <= p90
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bls_oews WHERE wage_annual_p10 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p25 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_median IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p75 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p90 IS NOT NULL AND NOT (wage_annual_p10 <= wage_annual_p25 AND wage_annual_p25 <= wage_annual_median AND wage_annual_median <= wage_annual_p75 AND wage_annual_p75 <= wage_annual_p90)
rules.description: Where all five annual percentiles are non-null, p10 <= p25 <= median <= p75 <= p90 must hold. Mathematical invariant of percentile ordering. A violation indicates a parsing error (column shift, value swap) or top-code mishandling.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 1 DQ Rules: 'wage_annual_p25 <= wage_annual_median <= wage_annual_p75 for every row where all three are non-null (P0 -- monotonicity).' Extended in this rule to the full chain (p10..p90) per EDA. EDA §Cross-Field Analysis: 'On all 826 rows with full annual percentile data, p10 <= p25 <= median <= p75 <= p90 holds. Zero violations.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: wage_capped = True implies at least one annual percentile = 239200
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bls_oews WHERE wage_capped = TRUE AND NOT (wage_annual_p10 = 239200 OR wage_annual_p25 = 239200 OR wage_annual_median = 239200 OR wage_annual_p75 = 239200 OR wage_annual_p90 = 239200)
rules.description: Every row with wage_capped=True must have at least one annual percentile equal to the BLS top-code floor of $239,200. A True flag without a 239200 value indicates a flag-derivation bug.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 1 DQ Rules: 'wage_capped = True only when at least one wage field = 239200 (P0).' EDA: 'No row is flagged wage_capped without at least one annual percentile equal to 239200.' 45 capped SOCs all satisfy this. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: wage_capped = False implies no annual percentile = 239200 (false-positive guard)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bls_oews WHERE wage_capped = FALSE AND (wage_annual_p10 = 239200 OR wage_annual_p25 = 239200 OR wage_annual_median = 239200 OR wage_annual_p75 = 239200 OR wage_annual_p90 = 239200)
rules.description: Companion to RAW-OEWS-006. Any row with an annual percentile at the BLS top-code floor of $239,200 must have wage_capped=True. A 239200 value without the flag indicates the ingestor missed the '#' sentinel during parsing.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 1 DQ Rules implies a biconditional via the iff-style invariant in the OOH analog (RAW-OOH-004). EDA: 0 false-positives observed -- every 239200 value has wage_capped=True. This rule guards the reverse direction so that future BLS edge cases (e.g. an exact-numeric $239,200 that is NOT a top-code) cannot silently slip in. Threshold: 0 violations.
rules.evidence_link: governance/eda/raw-bls-oews-eda.md §Field Profiles -> wage_capped (cross-check: 0 rows with 239200 lacking the flag)

rules.name: Spot check: SOC 15-1252 (Software Developers) median in [110000, 150000]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bls_oews WHERE soc_code = '15-1252' AND (wage_annual_median IS NULL OR wage_annual_median < 110000 OR wage_annual_median > 150000)
rules.description: SOC 15-1252 (Software Developers) is a known-good high-volume occupation. Its annual median must fall within [$110K, $150K]. A miss indicates row-shift, column-swap, or row-loss during ingest.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 1 DQ Rules: 'Software Developers (15-1252) median between $110K-$150K (P0).' EDA: 'median = $133,080, p25 = $103,050, p75 = $169,000, p90 = $211,450, capped=False. PASS -- well inside DQ window.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Spot check: SOC 29-1141 (Registered Nurses) median in [75000, 100000]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bls_oews WHERE soc_code = '29-1141' AND (wage_annual_median IS NULL OR wage_annual_median < 75000 OR wage_annual_median > 100000)
rules.description: SOC 29-1141 (Registered Nurses) is the largest healthcare occupation by employment (~3.3M workers) and a known-good calibration anchor. Its annual median must fall within [$75K, $100K].
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 1 DQ Rules: 'Registered Nurses (29-1141) median between $75K-$100K (P0).' EDA: 'median $93,600, p25 $78,610, p75 $107,960, capped=False. PASS -- median above spec calibration but inside Bronze DQ window. May 2024 vintage shifted upward.' The window accommodates the May 2024 vintage drift without a spec edit. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: occupation_title not null: 100%
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bls_oews WHERE occupation_title IS NULL OR TRIM(occupation_title) = ''
rules.description: Every occupation must have a non-null, non-empty title. Required for human display and used downstream in the data dictionary and lineage records.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 1 DQ Rules: 'occupation_title non-null: 100% (P0).' Spec schema marks occupation_title as required (yes). EDA: 'Null Rate: 0.0%. Cardinality: 831 distinct titles (100% unique).' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: All non-null annual wage values are >= 0 (non-negative wage guard)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bls_oews WHERE (wage_annual_p10 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p10 < 0) OR (wage_annual_p25 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p25 < 0) OR (wage_annual_median IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_median < 0) OR (wage_annual_p75 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p75 < 0) OR (wage_annual_p90 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p90 < 0) OR (wage_annual_mean IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_mean < 0)
rules.description: For every row, every non-null annual wage column (wage_annual_p10, wage_annual_p25, wage_annual_median, wage_annual_p75, wage_annual_p90, wage_annual_mean) must be >= 0. A negative wage is impossible in the BLS OEWS domain (annual compensation cannot be negative). Closes chaos scenario S10, which set wage_annual_p25 = -5000 on SOC 11-1021 while preserving monotonicity and went undetected by the original rule set.
rules.rationale: Structural domain guard surfaced by the chaos-monkey post-mortem (5 cycles, 9/10 caught; gap on S10). EDA confirms real data has no negative wages today -- the smallest published non-suppressed wage is $30,160 (per EDA §Field Profiles). RAW-OEWS-005 (monotonicity) is satisfied by any uniformly-shifted-down chain, so it does not catch a single negative value injected at p25 if the inserter preserved p10 <= p25 <= median (e.g. p10 also driven negative or already null). RAW-OEWS-008/009 only spot-check two SOCs. This rule closes the gap structurally for all rows and all six annual wage columns. Threshold: 0 violations (100% of rows must satisfy).

rules.name: wage_capped=TRUE row count in [5, 80] (top-code-floor drift detector)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 5 OR cnt > 80 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM bronze.bls_oews WHERE wage_capped = TRUE)
rules.description: The number of rows with wage_capped=TRUE must be between 5 and 80 inclusive. Detects future BLS top-code-floor changes: if BLS raises the $239,200 ceiling in a future vintage, the capped count will collapse toward zero; if the ingestor regresses on `#` parsing, the count will collapse to zero or balloon out of range. Also flags any sudden expansion of high-wage SOC populations.
rules.rationale: EDA §Edge Cases for DQ Thresholds, row 4: 'Top-coded SOCs (#): 45 rows in May 2024 vintage.' P1 (not P0) because the boundary is empirical and reasonable drift is allowed -- 45 sits comfortably inside [5, 80]. The lower bound 5 catches catastrophic ingestor regression on the `#` sentinel; the upper bound 80 catches a doubling of capped-SOC population, which would indicate either a BLS methodology shift (warrants spec re-evaluation) or a parser bug treating non-capped SOCs as capped. Surfaced by the chaos-monkey post-mortem: monitoring the capped population is a structural complement to the per-row biconditional in RAW-OEWS-006/007.
rules.evidence_link: governance/eda/raw-bls-oews-eda.md §Edge Cases for DQ Thresholds (row 4); governance/audit-trail/2026-05-06-dq-rule-writer-bls-oews.md §Post-Chaos Addendum

rules.name: total_employment non-negative AND non-null rate >= 99%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN neg_count > 0 OR (CAST(non_null AS DOUBLE) / CAST(total AS DOUBLE)) < 0.99 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, SUM(CASE WHEN total_employment IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS non_null, SUM(CASE WHEN total_employment IS NOT NULL AND total_employment < 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS neg_count FROM bronze.bls_oews)
rules.description: Every non-null `total_employment` value must be >= 0, AND the non-null rate must be >= 99%. `total_employment` is the second numeric payload column in the table and previously had ZERO rules of any kind. The ingestor's `_coerce_employment()` method (`src/raw/bls_oews_ingestor.py` line 401-424) accepts negative integers and absurdly large values without sign or magnitude checks. Closes adversarial-auditor gap G1 -- the structural mirror of the negative-wage attack (S10/RAW-OEWS-011) re-pointed at total_employment.
rules.rationale: Adversarial audit gap G1 (P0). EDA §Field Profiles -> total_employment: 100% non-null in May 2024 vintage; max value 3.28M (Retail Salespersons); min value > 0. Non-null floor of 99% leaves a 1% (~8 rows) buffer for future suppression while detecting catastrophic regression. Non-negativity is a structural domain invariant: an employment count cannot be negative. Threshold: 0 violations, 100% pass against current data. EDA recommended this rule on line 61 ('Consider P1 sanity rule total_employment >= 0 if not already present') -- elevated to P0 per audit because it is the same severity class as the negative-wage gap.

rules.name: Annual wage upper-bound sanity (percentiles <= 239200; mean <= 500000)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM bronze.bls_oews WHERE (wage_annual_p10 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p10 > 239200) OR (wage_annual_p25 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p25 > 239200) OR (wage_annual_median IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_median > 239200) OR (wage_annual_p75 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p75 > 239200) OR (wage_annual_p90 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p90 > 239200) OR (wage_annual_mean IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_mean > 500000)
rules.description: Annual percentile fields (`wage_annual_p10`, `wage_annual_p25`, `wage_annual_median`, `wage_annual_p75`, `wage_annual_p90`) are bounded above by the BLS top-code floor of $239,200 by definition -- BLS caps every published percentile at this ceiling. Any value above $239,200 in a percentile field indicates a parser bug (e.g., a x1000 multiplication that preserves monotonicity), a column shift, or a top-code mishandle. `wage_annual_mean` is published uncapped but bounded empirically by the EDA-observed maximum ($432,490 for Cardiologists); $500,000 sets a 16% headroom above observed max to catch order-of-magnitude regressions. Closes adversarial-auditor gap G2 -- the positive-direction twin of the negative-wage rule (RAW-OEWS-011).
rules.rationale: Adversarial audit gap G2 (P0). The BLS top-code floor of $239,200 is a published methodology constant -- 45 SOCs in May 2024 are floored at exactly this value (per RAW-OEWS-006/007). EDA §Field Profiles confirms max non-cap percentile = $216,800 and max mean = $432,490 (Cardiologists). A x1000 parser bug would preserve monotonicity (RAW-OEWS-005) and would survive the spot checks (RAW-OEWS-008/009 cover only 2 of 831 SOCs). The two ceilings ($239,200 percentiles, $500,000 mean) are the structural domain bounds. Threshold: 0 violations -- passes cleanly against current real Bronze data per EDA evidence.

rules.name: Detailed-only filter held: no summary-group SOC codes present
rules.description: No row's `soc_code` may match an OEWS summary-group pattern: `XX-0000` (major group), `XX-X000` (minor group), or `XX-XX00` (broad group). The ingestor filters source rows to `OCC_GROUP == 'detailed'` (`src/raw/bls_oews_ingestor.py` line 226). If that filter regresses (case-sensitivity break, encoding change, column rename), summary-group rows could leak into Bronze with populated wage fields and would silently join to nothing in OOH/O*NET. The row-count band [800, 900] (RAW-OEWS-001) only catches catastrophic filter failure; a partial leakage that lands inside the band would pass undetected. Closes adversarial-auditor gap G3.
rules.rationale: Adversarial audit gap G3 (P0). SOC 2018 standard reserves the `XX-0000`, `XX-X000`, and `XX-XX00` patterns for summary-group rollups (major, minor, broad respectively). Detailed occupations always have a non-zero last two digits (e.g., `15-1252` Software Developers, `29-1141` Registered Nurses). EDA confirms all 831 rows in current Bronze are detailed; zero match the summary-group regex. This rule is structurally cleaner than amending the schema to passthrough `OCC_GROUP` (which would require a spec amendment per the 'no schema changes without a spec' rule). Threshold: 0 violations -- passes cleanly against current real Bronze.
